prompt: We're adding multi-tenancy to our SaaS analytics platform. Currently it's single-tenant — one deployment per customer on separate subdomains (acme.analytics.io, globex.analytics.io). We want to move to a shared infrastructure model where all tenants share the same database and application instances but data is isolated. The stack is: Next.js frontend, FastAPI backend, PostgreSQL database, Redis for caching, and Celery for async jobs. We have about 40 tables, ~200 API endpoints, and 15 background job types. Current customer count is 23 but we're targeting 500+ in the next year. We use Alembic for migrations, pytest for backend tests, and Cypress for e2e.

assertions.text: Output contains a Decomposition Summary section with Goal, Affected Areas, Workstreams, Risk, and Execution Waves
Tasks are organized into execution waves with clear sequencing and dependency references
Every task card includes at minimum: Goal, In Scope, Non-Goals, Dependencies, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands
Each task card has a single primary purpose — no card combines unrelated concerns
Includes discovery cards to audit the 40 tables, 200 endpoints, and/or 15 job types before implementation
Explicitly addresses data isolation strategy (row-level security, tenant_id column, or application-level filtering)
Covers all layers: database schema, API middleware/tenant resolution, cache isolation (Redis), background jobs (Celery), and frontend
Identifies this as a high-risk change and includes appropriate rollback/rollout notes
Output includes a Follow-up Tasks section (even if 'None')
